text.title: Usability Test Session Tracker Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Usability Test Session Tracker Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One tracker artefact MUST represent exactly one session with one participant. Batching multiple participants into one tracker is rejected.
rule.rationale: A per-session record keeps task outcomes traceable to the participant; aggregation happens downstream, never at logging time.

rule.statement: Every task outcome MUST be one of: success | partial | fail | abandoned. Free-form prose is rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed outcomes make downstream aggregation deterministic; free-form notes do not aggregate.

rule.statement: The tracker MUST name a single accountable moderator (name + email or org handle). "team" / "we" are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single throat-to-choke; future readers need a named human to chase if the data is ambiguous.

rule.statement: Tracker MUST carry semver version and last_reviewed ISO date. Stale records (>90 days no review) are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; versioning + review-by-date forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: Every observed friction note MUST cite the task id and the verbatim participant utterance (paraphrase is rejected).
rule.rationale: Verbatim utterances are the falsifiability anchor; paraphrased frustration is unfalsifiable.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
